user.title: Connect Wiser by Feller µGateway
user.description: Enter the IP address of your µGateway and click submit. The buttons should start to flash pink and purple. Press any of the flashing buttons on the µGateway within 30 seconds to connect your Home Assistant instance. After that, depending on the amount and generation of devices in your Wiser network, the initial setup can take up to two minutes.

data_description.host: The hostname or IP address of your µGateway.
data_description.username: The username to be used for the API. You can enter anything, however only one connection per username is possible. If you have multiple instances, use unique usernames.
data_description.import_user: The user to import existing Wiser configuration from (like scenes, rooms and load names). If you don't have any other systems using the API, you can choose between 'installer' (Wiser eSetup app) and 'admin' (Wiser Home app).

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Wiser by Feller µGateway
reconfigure.description: Update the connection details for your µGateway. Press any flashing button on the device within 30 seconds to reconnect.

data_description.host: The new hostname or IP address of your µGateway.
data_description.username: The username to be used for the API.
data_description.import_user: The user to import existing Wiser configuration from.

reauth_confirm.title: Reconnect Wiser by Feller µGateway
reauth_confirm.description: Enter the IP address of your µGateway and click submit. The buttons should start to flash pink and purple. Press any of the flashing buttons on the µGateway within 30 seconds to connect your Home Assistant instance.

data_description.host: The hostname or IP address of your µGateway.
data_description.username: The username to be used for the API. You can enter anything, however only one connection per username is possible. If you have multiple instances, use unique usernames.
data_description.import_user: The user to import existing Wiser configuration from (like scenes, rooms and load names). If you don't have any other systems using the API, you can choose between 'installer' (Wiser eSetup app) and 'admin' (Wiser Home app).

abort.all_configured: All Wiser µGateways are already configured
abort.already_in_progress: Configuration flow is already in progress
abort.not_wiser_gateway: The device you're trying to connect to is not a Wiser µGateway. Are you sure you've entered the correct hostname or IP?
abort.wrong_device: The device at the new address has a different serial number. Use this flow only to update the address of the same µGateway.

error.invalid_import_user: Could not import information from this user. If you never connected to your system, use user 'installer' instead of 'admin'.
error.register_failed: Failed to register, please try again
error.connection_timeout: Connection timeout while trying to connect. Please check if the hostname or IP is accessible.
error.no_site_info: The API returned the error 'no site info'. This means your system has not been finalized yet. Please review the FAQ section of the integration for more information.

data_description.allow_missing_gateway_data: By default, the setup fails if fields like `fw_version` or `serial_nr` are missing for devices in the API response. Enable this option for debug purposes to disable the check. WARNING: Use with caution, this can affect entity IDs and functionality!

options.0: Primary (Up/Plus for dimmer or motor buttons)
options.1: Down / Minus (only for dimmer or motor buttons)

missing_device_data.title: Incomplete device data for device {device_id}
missing_device_data.description:
The µGateway returned incomplete data for device **{device_id}** (missing fields like serial number or firmware version). This can cause non-unique device identifiers and has been documented in [Feller-AG/wiser-api#43](https://github.com/Feller-AG/wiser-api/issues/43). The integration has been paused to prevent data corruption.

Open this repair to resolve it.

init.title: Incomplete device data
init.description:
The µGateway returned incomplete data for device **{device_id}** (missing fields like serial number or firmware version). This can cause non-unique device identifiers and has been documented in [Feller-AG/wiser-api#43](https://github.com/Feller-AG/wiser-api/issues/43). The integration has been paused to prevent data corruption.

The fix asks the µGateway to re-read the device and then reloads the integration. Click **Next** to review the fix, or close this dialog to do nothing.

confirm.title: Refresh device data
confirm.description:
Click **Fix** to request a property refresh for device **{device_id}** from the µGateway. This can take a few seconds. The integration will reload automatically if the refresh succeeds.

If this fix does not work (e.g. with pre-production devices), you can enable the **Allow missing µGateway data** option in the integration settings to bypass this check. This can have unintended side-effects, so only do this if you know what you are doing.

reload.title: Incomplete device data
reload.description:
The µGateway returned incomplete data for device **{device_id}** (missing fields like serial number or firmware version), which can cause non-unique device identifiers. See [issue #48](https://github.com/Syonix/ha-wiser-by-feller/issues/48) and [Feller-AG/wiser-api#43](https://github.com/Feller-AG/wiser-api/issues/43) for details.

Your µGateway firmware is older than **6.0.40**, so the integration cannot trigger an automatic data refresh. To resolve this, try one of the following:

- **Update the µGateway firmware** to 6.0.40 or newer, then reopen this repair to use the automatic fix.
- **Power-cycle the affected device** so the µGateway re-reads its data.
- Enable **Allow missing µGateway data** in the integration settings to bypass this check (only if you know what you are doing).

After trying one of the above, click **Reload** to reload the integration and re-check the device data.

error.refresh_failed: The µGateway did not accept the refresh request. Please try again or check µGateway connectivity.
error.reload_failed: The refresh request was sent, but the µGateway still reports incomplete data. The issue may have reappeared. Please try again or check the µGateway.
error.reload_failed_legacy: The device data is still incomplete after reloading. Update the µGateway firmware to 6.0.40 or newer, power-cycle the affected device, or enable the "Allow missing µGateway data" option in the integration settings.

abort.missing_data: Repair metadata is incomplete — cannot determine which device to fix.
abort.entry_not_found: The µGateway configuration entry was not found.
abort.no_coordinator: The µGateway coordinator is not available.

unknown_load_type.title: Unknown load type "{item_type}" on load {item_id}
unknown_load_type.description:
Load "{item_name}" (ID: {item_id}) has an unknown type "{item_type}" and is not supported by this integration. It will be ignored until support is added.

If you are using pre-production devices, please report this load type to the integration maintainers so it can be added.

unknown_sensor_type.title: Unknown sensor type "{item_type}" on sensor {item_id}
unknown_sensor_type.description:
Sensor "{item_name}" (ID: {item_id}) has an unknown type "{item_type}" and is not supported by this integration. It will be ignored until support is added.

If you are using pre-production devices, please report this sensor type to the integration maintainers so it can be added.

invalid_channel.message: Channel {channel} does not exist on this device.

multiple_wlan_devices.message: Multiple WLAN devices (gateways) found in the same K+ network: {first} and {second}. Only one µGateway per network is supported.

no_gateway_loaded.message: No Wiser µGateway is currently loaded.

specify_gateway.message: Multiple µGateways are configured. Select which one in the µGateway field.

unmanaged_button.message: This button is unmanaged. See the LED Control documentation for more information: https://github.com/Syonix/ha-wiser-by-feller/blob/main/docs/led-control.md

firmware_too_old.message: This feature requires µGateway firmware {min_version} or newer. Your gateway is running {current_version}.

status_light.name: Configure status light
status_light.description: Configure the status light color and brightness for a Wiser by Feller device.

device.name: Device
device.description: The device (load or scene / secondary control unit).

color.name: Color
color.description: The RGB color to display on the status light.

color_off.name: Color when off
color_off.description: The RGB color to display on the status light when the load is turned off. When unchecked, the "Color" value is used.

channel.name: Channel
channel.description: The specific button on the device to control.

brightness_on.name: Brightness when on
brightness_on.description: Button illumination brightness level when the load is turned on (0–100).

brightness_off.name: Brightness when off
brightness_off.description: Button illumination brightness level when the load is turned off (0–100). When unchecked, the "Brightness when on" value is used.

set_button_led_override.name: Set Button LED Override
set_button_led_override.description: Set a temporary LED override on a Wiser button LED.

config_entry_id.name: µGateway
config_entry_id.description: The µGateway the button belongs to. Optional with a single gateway; required when multiple are configured.

button_id.name: Button ID
button_id.description: Wiser button ID. Use the Find Button service to discover it.

led_index.name: LED
led_index.description: Which LED to control on the button.

clear_button_led_override.name: Clear Button LED Override
clear_button_led_override.description: Disable the temporary LED override and return to the configured Wiser LED behavior.

config_entry_id.name: µGateway
config_entry_id.description: The µGateway the button belongs to. Optional with a single gateway; required when multiple are configured.

button_id.name: Button ID
button_id.description: Wiser button ID. Use the Find Button service to discover it.

led_index.name: LED
led_index.description: Which LED to control on the button.

find_button.name: Find Button
find_button.description: Activates find-me mode: all button LEDs start blinking. Press any physical button to identify it. The button ID and other information are returned.

config_entry_id.name: µGateway
config_entry_id.description: The µGateway to activate find-me mode on. Optional with a single gateway; required when multiple are configured.
